case.paper_title: A Business by Any Other Name: Firm Name Choice as a Signal of Firm Quality
case.focal_claim: The average plumbing firm whose name begins with A or a number receives more service complaints than other firms.

original_datasets.name: final_data

summary_statistics.info: 2293 rows x 19 columns. Dtypes: 18 float32, 1 object. Key non-null counts: firm_id (2293), complaints_2008 (2293), first_A (2293), ad_spend_k (2293), multiple_names (2293), chicago (2293), emp_size (2293), firm_age (2293); some price variables have 543 non-missing.

original_datasets.notes: Illinois plumbing firms dataset. Outcome is count of complaints in 2008. Binary predictor first_A indicates firm names beginning with 'A' or a number. Control variables include ad_spend_k, firm_age, chicago, emp_size, multiple_names.

tasks.task_instruction: Conduct the planned comparisons and count models of complaints on first_A with and without controls.

analysis_path.path_description: Import the Illinois plumbing dataset; test differences in complaints by first_A; fit logit for any complaint; fit Poisson, zero-inflated Poisson, and negative binomial models of complaint counts with and without controls; compute margins where applicable.
preprocessing_steps: Run tests of association and fit regression models.

key_choices.outcome_measure: complaints_2008 (count of BBB complaints in 2008); complaints_2008_bin for binary models.
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: first_A (indicator for firm names beginning with 'A' or a number).
key_choices.data_processing: Create binary outcome for any complaint; use controls as listed in multivariate models.
key_choices.inference_rule: Coefficient z/t-tests and p-values; margins at means for selected models.

outcome.name: complaints_2008
outcome.definition: Count of customer complaints recorded in 2008; binary variant complaints_2008_bin = complaints_2008 > 0 for logit.

main_predictor.name: first_A
main_predictor.definition: Indicator for firm names beginning with 'A' or a number.

controls.name: multiple_names
controls.definition: Indicator for firms using multiple names.

analysis_code.notes: Translated from Stata do-file (replication.do) to Python. Script prints a JSON summary to stdout.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Follow this planned path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

tasks.task_instruction: Produce a single main result using standard test statistics; use overall complaints and do not restrict to Chicago-only firms or online-review outcomes.

analysis_path.path_description: Import the dataset; run a Welch t-test of complaints by first_A and a negative binomial regression of complaints_2008 on first_A; report the z-statistic and p-value for first_A.
preprocessing_steps: Run Welch t-test of complaints_2008 by first_A.
Fit negative binomial GLM: complaints_2008 ~ first_A and extract z-statistic.

key_choices.main_predictor_measure: first_A (indicator for firm names beginning with 'A' or a number).
key_choices.data_processing: No additional processing beyond constructing group subsets for t-test.
key_choices.inference_rule: z-statistic and p-value for first_A coefficient; Welch t-test p-value.

outcome.name: complaints_2008
outcome.definition: Count of customer complaints recorded in 2008.

main_predictor.name: first_A
main_predictor.definition: Indicator for firm names beginning with 'A' or a number.

analysis_code.notes: Translated from Stata do-file (replication_task2.do) to Python. Script prints a JSON summary including t-test and NB z-statistics.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Use the planned negative binomial model for the main result; do not substitute outcomes from online review sites.

files.data/replication__py.py: Python translation of replication.do. Loads final_data.dta, runs t-test, chi-square, logit, Poisson, ZIP with margins, and negative binomial with margins; prints JSON.
files.data/replication_task2__py.py: Python translation of replication_task2.do. Runs Welch t-test and a negative binomial GLM on complaints_2008 ~ first_A; prints JSON with key test statistics.

codebase.notes: Non-Python scripts were translated to Python per policy. All file I/O points to /app/data. The Stata do-files are not executed directly.

analysis.instructions: Execute Task1 and Task2 using the provided Python scripts against the original dataset and capture the printed JSON outputs for standardized result extraction.
not_allowed_actions: replace the planned path with a different analysis path

debugging_rule.repair_scope: fix dependency, file path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Group means and Welch t-test for complaints_2008 by first_A; chi-square test for binary complaints; coefficients, standard errors, z/t-statistics, and p-values for first_A in logit, Poisson, ZIP, and negative binomial models; margins at means for selected models.
